# CircleCI CLI

Work with CircleCI trigger events.

An event is the record of a trigger firing — a push, an API call,
or a schedule. It groups the workflows started by that firing,
each of which contains jobs.

## Usage

`circleci event <command> [flags]`

## Available Commands

| Command  | Description                                                |
| -------- | ---------------------------------------------------------- |
| `cancel` | Cancel an event                                            |
| `create` | Create a new event (start workflows)                       |
| `get`    | Get an event's status                                      |
| `list`   | List recent events for a project                           |
| `open`   | Open the current project's appropriate page in the browser |
| `watch`  | Watch an event until its workflows complete                |

## Inherited Flags

| Flag                  | Description                                                  |
| --------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------ |
| `-c, --config string` | path to config file (default: ~/.config/circleci/config.yml) |
| `--debug`             | enable debug logging                                         |
| `-q, --quiet`         | suppress informational output; data on stdout is unaffected  |

## Learn More

- Use `circleci <command> <subcommand> --help` for more information about a command.
- Read the manual at <https://circleci.com/docs/local-cli>
- Support at <https://github.com/CircleCI-Public/circleci-cli/issues>
